author: Courtney Dunnegan
text: Our 13 year old has been taking lessons for 9 months with 2 other students through zoom with Mr. Rosales. He can speak sentences in Spanish now with no prior knowledge. Mr. Rosales keeps the group on task while teaching and keeping the kids learning while having fun! They play interactive games, read Spanish novels, learn through music videos, and even practice writing in Spanish. Mr. Rosales has great communication through email and starts class on time!

author: Emily Phylpo
text: Rosales is such an amazing teacher who always puts his students best interest first. Learning is both comfortable and understandable! 🤩 He always made learning a new language both fun and easy, and almost a decade after being taught by him, a lot has stuck. He is the guy to go to if you’re wanting to dive into Spanish!

author: Maggie Miller
text: Mr. Rosales was my high school Spanish teacher at Annawan High School for 3 years. His teaching skills are very well thought out, planned, articulate, and easy to go along with while learning. He also makes sure that if you don't quite understand the materials to go over them again with you as needed. He uses flashcards to help students build their memorization skills. His tests make sure you know the materials and content so if you needed to go over the materials some more his tests will show that. While I was there he even chaperoned a trip to Spain with some of the students in Spanish club and those that had taken Spanish. While we were in Spain we used our Spanish skills to read and communicate. I highly recommend Mr. Rosales to anyone looking to learn the Spanish language from as well as other cultural education as he taught me a lot in those three years and is one of my favorite teachers. Thank you, Mr. Rosales! For being a great teacher and for teaching in a way that makes it easy for everyone to learn with your wide variety of educational activities throughout your courses. It really made a difference. I still use the knowledge that I remember mostly with reading and listening.

author: Lucas Fehlberg
text: Mr. Rosales was my Spanish teacher for two years. He helped me understand Spanish easily with help of repetition, especially when it came to making stories. We would repeat the story every morning, then proceed to add on to said story making it longer. This allowed for understanding of the material and learning as to what we were talking about in our story. I highly recommend Mr. Rosales.

author: Alan Yepsen
text: I decided a few years ago that I wanted to revisit learning Spanish. Something I hadn’t done since high school. My objective was and is, to incorporate Spanish into my office to see people that can’t seek care elsewhere because of the language barrier. I started doing the Duolingo App on my phone. I did lessons for about a year and knew I needed additional guidance. Lessons on the App always led to as many questions as answers. I looked at the local college and felt like their curriculum didn’t match my need. My wife came across Michael’s FB page and through reading his course structure, it sounded exactly what I was looking for. A direct way to learn and ask questions. Working at a pace that fits into my lifestyle by a patient instructor that has taught both in the classroom and with individuals in the past. Michaels classes were more than I expected. With his background in teaching in the classroom, he had practice lessons prepared ahead of time and computer programs with numerous resources that you could practice outside of class times. I have been doing lessons weekly with Michael for a year and will continue doing so. He is an honest, educated, caring person that motivates you to become better. Give him a try. You’ll be glad you did.

author: Jeff Brace
text: I have tried to learn Spanish in the past by prerecorded lessons and never felt I was getting anywhere. In high school, I took 3 years of French but never used it and now find it difficult to remember much of anything I learned. Michaels zoom classes are very engaging and with other students have found that in this format it has motivated me to use Spanish more and able to progress in understanding Spanish when spoken by others. I have found that each week builds on what is learned the previous weeks and encourages better retention of the language. Also, the different approaches; story building, reading, and translating Spanish story books, and the use of games (yeh battleship) have helped much in holding interest and engaging in conversation. I highly recommend Rosales Cultural Lessons format to either refresh your Spanish or to learn Spanish for the first time.

author: Kaylee Celus
text: Mr. Rosales was my high school Spanish teacher and I learned a lot from him. He makes sure the students understand what they are learning. He has the students practice through slideshows and creating other stories. The students also learn through flash cards which help to remember the words.
